title: Telanthophora arborescens
taxonomy: Telanthophora arborescens is a flowering plant species within the family Asteraceae. This genus is native to the mountainous regions of South America, particularly in the Andes. Telanthophora arborescens is classified as a shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 3 meters tall. The classification of this plant is further supported by its unique leaf and flower structures. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Telanthophora arborescens shares characteristics with other flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers.

parts: Telanthophora arborescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Chilean fire bush.  The plant's leaves and flowers are the primary parts used for medicinal purposes.
